programmes.show_description: A woman hires her housekeeper to clean her entire house in exchange for furniture in her storage unit, but claims she took the wrong items. S24, Ep24
A woman claims her friend loves to donate money to help care for dogs in distress, so she owes her nothing. S24, Ep25
A real estate broker plays a voice message that clearly states that her web designer agrees to return her deposit. S24, Ep26
A woman claims she was shot in the eye with a liquid sprayer at a grocery store and is still suffering; she sues for damages. S22, Ep151
A woman is accused of stealing money from her deceased mother's savings account; her stepfather sues her for payback. S22, Ep152
Children's visits to prison to see their convicted father are at the heart of a case between his sister and the daycare provider who escorted them. S22, Ep153
A man returns home to find his beloved Dachshund maimed and bleeding in a neighbour's backyard. S22, Ep154
A landlord accuses his teen tenant's boyfriend of head-butting him during an altercation; when he pushes back, the police are called. S22, Ep155
Concerned about the fate of a Pomeranian puppy, a breeder drives 6 hours to rescue the animal from an allegedly negligent pet owner. S22, Ep156
A woman being sued by her ex-lover for the return of his belongings claims she had to sell them to survive. S22, Ep157
The alleged death of a woman and her baby in a head-on collision is called into question when a social worker sues an aspiring singer for check fraud. S16, Ep56
Bruce Kellogg is shot four times while sleeping. The shooter is 19-year-old Denver McDowell who says he was put up to it by the victim's wife - 26-year-old Laurie Kellogg. S4, Ep3
A teacher out for a walk with her dog risks her own life to save her pet. S3, Ep2
When a mountain biker suffers a freak accident, a total stranger comes to his aid. S3, Ep3
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
Two Chihuahuas are found dead after a neighbour's American Bullies allegedly bust through a doggie door and attack them. S22, Ep123
A woman accuses her landlord of inappropriate behaviour; she denies owing him for rent and breaking her lease. S22, Ep124
The Judge discovers an intriguing story of alleged fraud and vandalism when an unsuspecting motorist calls for help. S22, Ep125
After a couple is asked to pay up for an unauthorised tenant, they complain about leaks, mould and sewage issues. S22, Ep126
A woman claims she sent racy pictures of herself in a negligee with another man to her ex-boyfriend and that's why she's being sued for his impounded car which she totalled. S16, Ep10
Inspection of a man's handwriting, defamation of character and an alleged assault come into play when ex-friends fight over repossession of a truck. S16, Ep11
A home-based Internet business, a divorce, and an ex-employee caught in the middle come into play when ex-roommates fight over rent. S16, Ep12
Claiming she made over $7,000 in car payments, a young woman sues her ex-stepfather after he repossesses her vehicle. S16, Ep13
The Judge questions a woman's courtroom attitude when she is sued by her ex-best friend for the value of a car and her personal belongings. S24, Ep27
A woman admits that she only knew a man for 24 hours before she invited him home; she says the ex-con kidnapped her and forced her to co-sign on a truck loan. S24, Ep28
An allegedly scorned woman warns her ex's new girlfriend not to date a man for a multitude of reasons; he accuses her of trespassing and harassment. S24, Ep29
A woman sues an alleged slumlord for injuries she received while on his property; his defence is that she should have seen the hole. S22, Ep158
A barter agreement to purchase a motorcycle ends with an unhappy seller who claims he received stolen merchandise and four flat tires. S22, Ep159
A woman claims she was forced to euthanise her seven-week-old German Shepherd puppy; she sues the man who sold the animal to her. S22, Ep160
An expecting mother allows her boyfriend to send risqué photos of them together to his ex-girlfriend who allegedly punches her in the head. S22, Ep161
A woman sues her ex-fiancé for an alleged loan to pay off his debt; he claims he spent plenty of money on her when they were together. S22, Ep162
After a woman accuses her estranged husband of using corporal punishment on their children, he turns to a new 'girlfriend' for help with a divorce attorney. S22, Ep163
A woman asks her nanny for thousands of dollars in order to send her 8-year-old daughter to cheerleading camp; her nanny wants payback. S22, Ep164
A dog perishes in an apartment fire caused by the pet's owner; the renter then sues the landlord and contractor for damages to his belongings. S16, Ep26
A poker party ends in thousands of dollars in alleged damages when a married couple finds deep scratches on their hardwood floor; they blame a card dealer. S16, Ep27
A second DUI leaves a woman with 320 hours of community service to complete; she claims a pastor swindled her into free labour. S16, Ep28
A PTA president sues the father of a promising scholarship winner after he fails to get her on the bus for a prestigious tour of colleges. S16, Ep29
Shane Gilmer and Laura Sugden were planning a quiet night out when a horrific ambush shattered their lives. S2, Ep9
The hunt is on for a ruthless fantasist who has defrauded our NHS of thousands. S2, Ep2
When a young woman vanishes during one of the worst droughts in California history, detectives embark on a decades-long investigation to find a serial killer. S3, Ep21
In the US, a missing 15-year-old is found by cadaver dogs. In the UK, a mystery needs to be solved to identify a dismembered body in a suitcase. S1, Ep2
In 2024, Pamela Wittman, a 25-year-old from Indiana, was identified, solving the 1980 Daytona Beach cold case linked to a serial killer. S6, Ep1

A woman asks her nanny for thousands of dollars in order to send her 8-year-old daughter to cheerleading camp; her nanny wants payback. S22, Ep164
A man says he had a horrible vacation in Puerto Rico and sues his former friend for the cost of the trip; she claims he invited her and had romantic intentions. S16, Ep57
Dental records identify a decomposing body as 23-year-old Marine Sergeant Elihue Parker. The prosecution says Elihue's estranged wife Kimberly Parker ordered his execution. S4, Ep4
A family on a safari holiday is attacked by an elephant as they sit down to eat. S3, Ep6
A paraglider pilot plunges 6,000 feet and lands 60 feet up in the tree canopy. S3, Ep4
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
When a retired pest control worker sprays insect killer on a shared tree, his neighbours claim they suffered serious respiratory injury. S22, Ep130
A writer claims she submitted articles that her editor never paid her for; he says the writing was not factual, and that he wasn't paid by his clients. S22, Ep131
A woman demands thousands in payback for a valuable truck; her friend says she traded an antique, hand carved oak table for the vehicle. S22, Ep132
A daycare provider says her attempt to improve a playground resulted in substandard concrete work; she sues for damages. S22, Ep133
A senior citizen is accused of assaulting another senior at a Ladies' Auxiliary VFW meeting and is escorted out of her hotel room by police. She sues for damages. S16, Ep14
After meeting a mechanic at a bowling alley, a woman ends up suing him for allegedly selling her broken-down BMW without her permission; he claims he was forced to get rid of it. S16, Ep15
A mother's allegedly inappropriate behaviour with her daughter's fiancé, drinking problems and damage caused by Chihuahuas come into play in a fight over fishing poles. S16, Ep16
After ruining her credit, a woman demands that her boyfriend's adult son return the car she co-signed for him or give her money to pay it off. S16, Ep17
A woman claims her ex-boyfriend slashed her tyres but fails to prove a word of her story; he is still in the hot seat for alleged theft of her property. S24, Ep30
Sad news is delivered in the midst of a fight between ex-roommates over ruined credit and alleged identity theft. S24, Ep31
A legally-blind woman is court ordered to take anger management classes after slapping her husband; she sues the class owner for unpaid wages. S24, Ep32
A man claims he was attacked in a sober-living facility; his housemate says he was leaving the stove on overnight to heat the place. S22, Ep165
Two women with children by the same man go after each other in a fight over defamation and loss of income; a third potential baby mama comes into play. S22, Ep166
A single father claims he had no choice but to take heroic efforts to protect his home from Hurricane Irma and used public property to do so. S22, Ep167
The Judge reads a woman the riot act when she allegedly uses the family court system to evict a tenant by means of a questionable restraining order. S22, Ep168
When a German Shepherd puppy dies, pet owners blame the breeder; the Judge investigates allegations of parvo running rampant in the litter. S22, Ep169
A man denies having intimate relations with a woman; she claims he lived with her for two months and assaulted her daughter. S22, Ep170
A woman goes after her estranged daughter-in-law for thousands in unpaid loans; she is met with rage over an allegedly deadbeat husband. S22, Ep171
Afraid that her intoxicated friend was about to be raped, a woman claims she pushed through an already broken window to rescue her; she is sued for breaking the window. S16, Ep30
Accused of being a crook after allegedly selling a $500 rug for $7500, a carpet shop owner denies all charges and insists the married couple was satisfied at the time of their purchase. S16, Ep31
A teenager accuses a teacher of punching him in the chest during an incident at school; the teacher countersues for attorney fees and punitive damages. S16, Ep32
Teenagers brawl in a parking lot and the police are called in; damages from alleged kicking, spitting on and keying cars are disputed. S16, Ep33
In June 2010, taxi driver Derrick Bird went on one of the worst killing sprees in British history. He shot dead twelve people and injured many more. S2, Ep10
A wealthy manager is taking the NHS for a ride; and a tax fraudster claims to be a spy. S2, Ep3
As Hurricane Isabel approaches the Virginia Coast, the burned body of a woman is found. S3, Ep22
A couple set up a weapons business in West Virginia. All is not what it seems, leading to a fatal end. S1, Ep2
Found shot near Highway 67 in 1986, Maria Pilar Del Gadillo Carrillo remained unidentified until 2024, when authorities used genetic genealogy and reopened her case. S6, Ep2
